# Runtime prep status (server + assets)

This document records the runtime-prep readiness for milestones M1 (boot), M2 (title screen), and M3 (login), based on the SERVER and ASSETS bringup performed in this session. It is the single source of truth for the launch command and the deob `ADD`s to inject the moment the Mudclient compile (M0) lands.

> Note on client lineage: this is the **rev 233-235 J++ `rsclassic` client**, not the classic `mudclient`. Two plan assumptions were corrected during staging: `jagex.jag` does **not** need to be hand-built (it is content index 3), and the 8 bitmap fonts are **rasterized at runtime from AWT Helvetica** (the `.jf` files are dead for this client).

## 1. Readiness table

| Item | Status | Detail / where |
|---|---|---|
| Server build | READY | `core.jar` (38 MB) + `plugins.jar` (2.5 MB) at `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/openrsc/server/`. Compiled with **JDK 17** (`/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk`) via vendored Ant 1.10.5. Build log `/tmp/rsc-run/server-build.log`. All deps vendored — no network. |
| Server running | **PARTIAL** | **Not currently live.** PID 318415 has exited and nothing is listening on 43594/43494 (verified: `ss` shows no listener, TCP connect refused). The "background task keeps it alive" claim did not hold — the JVM is down. Run log `/tmp/rsc-run/server-run.log` confirms it started 22:41 then went idle. **Restartable in one command** (see §3, M3) — all build/config/keys persist. |
| RSA modulus | READY | Captured and verified two ways (startup log + ASN.1 of `client.pem`). 512-bit. Decimal↔hex correspondence re-verified this session. Persisted in `client.pem`/`server.pem` (survives restart). **`proto/v235/rsa.go` is STALE** and must be overridden (its `DefaultServerRSA` modulus differs — confirmed). |
| Account | READY (in-protocol) | `westworld.db` has **zero players** (no seeded `alex`). `want_packet_register: true` → create over the wire (register opcode → `CharacterCreateRequest`, `LoginPacketHandler` ~L738). Logins from `127.0.0.1` bypass rate/IP limits. Password = cradle `-password` / `WESTWORLD_PASSWORD`. First-login path = **register-then-login**. |
| jagex.jag | READY | **Already exists as content index 3** — `loadJagex()` ignores the name string and indexes by int `3`. `content3_5181c9f5` (4990 B) decompresses to a JAG containing `logo.tga` (281×85). CRC32 = `0x5181c9f5` = `contentcrcs[3]`, so the client's CRC check passes. Convenience byte-identical copy at `/tmp/rsc-run/cache/jagex.jag`. |
| Content packs | READY | All 12 packs (`content0..11`) + `contentcrcs` (58 B) staged verbatim under `/tmp/rsc-run/cache/`. Matched rev-235 set, all CRCs consistent; `verifyCrc` over the manifest passes. **All 12 indices must resolve to reach the title screen** (`loadGameConfig` loads the full sequence as one block) — all 12 are present. |
| Content host | READY (must be running) | `/tmp/rsc-run/content-host.py` + launcher `/tmp/rsc-run/start-content-host.sh` (default port 43594). Required, not optional: a vanilla `http.server` 404s the cache-buster manifest path `/contentcrcs<hex-millis>`; this handler collapses any `/contentcrcs*` → the 58-byte manifest and serves packs verbatim. Smoke-tested end-to-end. **Pre-stage + skip does NOT work** (`Buffer._junkArray` is only filled by `CacheUpdater.downloadAndVerifyCrcs` when `doUpdate > 20`), so the host must be up and `doUpdate > 20`. |

## 2. Exact captured values

**Server endpoint (live port):**
- Game TCP: `127.0.0.1:43594`  ← use this. The cradle default `-server localhost:43596` is **STALE**; pass `-server localhost:43594`.
- WebSocket: `127.0.0.1:43494`
- DB: SQLite at `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/openrsc/server/inc/sqlite/westworld.db` (schema-only, 0 players). No MySQL.
- Config: `westworld.conf` at `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/openrsc/server/westworld.conf` (`enforce_custom_client_version: false`, `custom_sprites: false`, version gate accepts rev-235).

**RSA public key** (raw RSA, `m^e mod n`, no PKCS#1 padding; matches `proto/v235/login.go` layout):
- exponent (decimal): `65537`
- modulus (decimal):
  `8470727801174954902989859055344934434282083179399207801708507751976321325965228952554034824402302678046886295251980280826867546707365065713308009848924031`
- modulus (hex, 512-bit):
  `a1bc0e158a70c86b8bccd31aeac822bae08c2fd25681cfd8ecb6fa85562b08945b8fe1157433a6126a39797e39d5af7dd19a912514841962878850dc2c00677f`
- Private key: `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/openrsc/server/server.pem`; public: `client.pem`. **WARNING: deleting the `.pem` files regenerates a new key on next boot (modulus changes).**

**Account:** none pre-seeded. Username/password chosen at register time (password from cradle `-password` / `WESTWORLD_PASSWORD`); username in XTEA block, password + ISAAC keys + nonces in RSA block; `127.0.0.1` bypasses limits.

**Staged assets under `/tmp/rsc-run`:**
- `/tmp/rsc-run/cache/` — `content0..11`, `contentcrcs`, plus `jagex.jag` (= content3, byte-identical).
- `/tmp/rsc-run/content-host.py` — static host with `/contentcrcs*` collapse + verbatim packs.
- `/tmp/rsc-run/start-content-host.sh` — host launcher (default port 43594).
- `/tmp/rsc-run/start-server.sh` — server launcher (JDK 17 + Ant `runserver -DconfFile=westworld`).
- `/tmp/rsc-run/server-info.txt`, `server-build.log`, `server-run.log` — server detail/logs.
- `/tmp/rsc-run/work/` — `jag.py`, `logo.tga`, `FontProbe.java` (verification tooling).

> Port collision note: both the content host and the game server default to **43594**. Run the content host on a **different port** (e.g. 8080) and point the client's content `BASE_URL` at that port, since 43594 is the game socket. (`startApplication` hardcodes content host `127.0.0.1`; only the port is free to choose.)

## 3. Remaining blockers per milestone + the step to clear each

**M1 — boot (window + logo + fonts via `loadJagex`):**
- Blocker: **M0 (Mudclient compile) not landed** — out of scope here; this is the only gate.
- Blocker: content host must be **running** so `loadResource("content3", …)` can fetch the logo and pass CRC.
- Clear it: start the host →
  `PORT=8080 /tmp/rsc-run/start-content-host.sh` (serves `/tmp/rsc-run/cache/`). AWT Helvetica fonts need no asset (verified resolvable headless under JDK 17).

**M2 — title / login screen (`loadGameConfig`):**
- Blocker: `loadGameConfig` loads the **full 12-index sequence** as one block before drawing and bails on any `fatalLoadError`; all 12 must resolve over HTTP. All 12 are staged, so the only requirement is the **host being up** (same as M1) with `doUpdate > 20` and `BASE_URL` pointed at it.
- Clear it: same host as M1; ensure `doUpdate > 20` so `_junkArray` (CRC table) is populated.

**M3 — login (register-then-login over the wire):**
- Blocker: **server is not currently running** (PARTIAL above). Restart it.
- Blocker: deob's `BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS` must be the value in §2, not the stale `rsa.go`.
- Blocker: no account exists — must **register** before/at first login.
- Clear it:
  1. Restart server: `/tmp/rsc-run/start-server.sh` (run in background); wait for log line `Game world is now online on TCP port 43594!` and confirm `ss -ltn | grep 43594`.
  2. Inject the RSA override (value in §2) and point the client at `localhost:43594`.
  3. First connection uses the **register** path (register opcode → `CharacterCreateRequest`), then login with the same creds; or insert a `players` row directly into `westworld.db`.

## 4. Ready-to-execute the moment M0 (Mudclient) lands

Everything below is pre-staged; on M0 landing, execute M1→M3 by (a) starting the two background services and (b) injecting these `ADD`s into the deob client launch.

**Start services (do first):**
```sh
# Game server (clears the M3 "server running" PARTIAL)
/tmp/rsc-run/start-server.sh          # background; wait for "online on TCP port 43594!"

# Content host on a NON-43594 port (clears M1/M2 asset fetch)
PORT=8080 /tmp/rsc-run/start-content-host.sh   # serves /tmp/rsc-run/cache verbatim
```

**Launch command (client):** run the compiled Mudclient with the cradle, overriding the stale defaults:
```sh
<run-mudclient> -server localhost:43594 -password "$WESTWORLD_PASSWORD"
```

**ADDs to inject into the deob client:**

1. **RSA override** — set `BitBuffer.RSA_MODULUS` (and exponent) to the fresh server key:
   - modulus (hex): `a1bc0e158a70c86b8bccd31aeac822bae08c2fd25681cfd8ecb6fa85562b08945b8fe1157433a6126a39797e39d5af7dd19a912514841962878850dc2c00677f`
   - exponent: `65537`
   - Also update `proto/v235/rsa.go` `DefaultServerRSA` to this modulus (currently STALE) if the Go cradle is in the path.

2. **Host / port** — game socket `127.0.0.1:43594` (NOT 43596). The cradle scenario scripts already pass `-server localhost:43594`.

3. **Content path / doUpdate** — content `BASE_URL = http://127.0.0.1:8080` (the host port chosen above; client hardcodes host `127.0.0.1`), and `doUpdate > 20` so `CacheUpdater.downloadAndVerifyCrcs` runs and fills `Buffer._junkArray` (the CRC table `loadResource` needs to build URLs and pass CRC checks). Do **not** use the pre-stage + skip path — it leaves `_junkArray` zeroed and `loadResource` requests `content3_0`, failing CRC.

4. **First login = register-then-login** — no seeded account; on first connect take the register path (or pre-insert a `players` row into `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/openrsc/server/inc/sqlite/westworld.db`). `127.0.0.1` bypasses rate/IP limits.

With services up and these four ADDs in place, M1 (logo+fonts), M2 (all-12-index title load), and M3 (RSA-encrypted register-then-login over 43594) are unblocked the moment the Mudclient binary exists.
